title: pai prompt document
description: A portable prompt document (specVersion pai.prompt.v1): model + params + optional input/output schemas + message templates with {{variable}} slots + tool interfaces + skills. Simple form: top-level system/user. General form: a messages list. Runs identically in pai-sdk (Python) and structured-ai-sdk (TypeScript).

specVersion.description: Prompt-document spec version. Optional on input (assumed pai.prompt.v1); always emitted on output.

name.description: Prompt name (identifies it in logs and traces).

model.description: provider/model-id string, e.g. 'anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5'. Optional — can be supplied at call time instead.

params.description: Call options in the AI SDK vocabulary (camelCase), passed to generateText/streamText verbatim in TypeScript and mapped 1:1 onto pai-sdk keyword arguments in Python. Per-call overrides win.

providerOptions.description: Provider-specific passthrough keyed by provider name (thinking/effort/reasoning config lives here). Contents are sent verbatim.

input.description: Structured input signature: either field-type shorthand ({field: string|number|integer|boolean|'<type>[]'|[enum,...]|nested mapping}) or a full JSON Schema via {schema: {...}}. Shorthand fields are all required; use full JSON Schema for optional fields.

schema.description: Full JSON Schema for the input object.

output.description: Structured output: either field-type shorthand ({field: string|number|integer|boolean|'<type>[]'|[enum,...]|nested mapping}) or a full JSON Schema via {schema: {...}}.

schema.description: Full JSON Schema for the output object.

system.description: Simple form: the system prompt template. Mutually exclusive with messages:.

user.description: Simple form: the user message template. Mutually exclusive with messages:.

messages.description: General form: explicit message list (multiple system blocks, few-shot assistant turns, stable ids). Mutually exclusive with top-level system/user.

tools.description: Tool interfaces, keyed by tool name. Behavior binds at call time via handlers={name: fn}; declared tools without a handler are client-side.

skills.description: Skills, keyed by name (letters, digits, '-', '_'; full match). Each renders as a system message with id 'skill:<name>' after the last declared system message, in code-point-sorted name order (key order is never semantic). description = when to apply (literal prose); instructions = how (template). Both are optimizer-addressable text; the name is the contract.

toolChoice.description: How the model may use the declared tools.

templateString.description: Text with Mustache-style {{variable}} placeholders. Plain {{name}} only; single braces are literal text.

content.description: Literal text — no interpolation, braces left alone.

id.description: Stable id for addressing this message (e.g. with_template).

outputFieldType.description: string | number | integer | boolean (null means string), '<type>[]' for arrays, a list of literals for an enum, or a nested mapping for a nested object.

description.description: What the tool does AND when to call it. Optimizer runs may target this description by tool name; the tool name and input schema are the contract and never change.

input.description: Input schema: field-type shorthand (same grammar as output:) or full JSON Schema via {schema: {...}}.

output.description: Declared result schema: field-type shorthand or full JSON Schema via {schema: {...}}. Interface documentation and typing data; not enforced against handler return values at run time.

description.description: When this skill applies. Literal prose (braces render as-is).

instructions.description: How to apply the skill. {{variables}} join the prompt's input contract.
